title: Write Boundary and Dry Run
outOfScope: Business source code, application CI, and production policy activation
Writing any file in the target repository before explicit confirmation
Adoption evidence generation and configuration readiness aggregation

sources.reason: Adoption write and dry-run behavior currently documented.
External session boundary consumed by the write planner.
Repository facts and drift revalidation used before writes.
Existing installer dry-run and Makefile write semantics.

riskAssessment.reason: The implementation is a standard-library planner that rejects out-of-scope writes and performs no I/O until explicit confirmation.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized implementation, tests, documentation, PR, merge, closure, and branch cleanup.

preReviewWarnings: Review path allowlist, dry-run zero writes, Makefile managed-block ownership, confirmation value, and fail-closed behavior.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A write plan exposes only explicitly allowed paths and rejects target paths outside the allowlist before I/O.
Dry-run and pre-confirmation execution perform zero filesystem writes and report planned actions.
A managed Makefile block appends or replaces content only through an exact marker and remains idempotent.
Non-interactive mode fails closed without an explicit confirmation value; confirmed execution revalidates repository drift before writing.

tests/test_bootstrap_write_boundary.py contains assertions for clean allowed writes, zero-write dry-run, traversal/symlink/marker rejection, drift rejection, non-interactive confirmation rejection, and idempotent managed Makefile updates; both adoption guides describe these boundaries.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.
No write is permitted outside the declared allowlist or without explicit confirmation.

intent.problem: Bootstrap needs a narrow, auditable write boundary: proposal and confirmation must not mutate the adopter, dry-run must be genuinely read-only, and Makefile integration must not overwrite project-owned content.
constraints: Keep the planner independent from business code and production policy activation.
Require a confirmed repository snapshot and an explicit confirmation value for writes; tests use local fixtures only.
Treat path traversal, symlinks, stale repository facts, and malformed managed markers as fail-closed conditions.
intent.rationale: A pure plan/execute boundary lets the wizard remain external and side-effect-free while making every later write inspectable, idempotent, and reversible.

rawUserRequest: 先走完当前文档的流程。用户已授权按计划逐工单执行，包含修改代码、测试、文档、PR、合并、ai-close-work-item、分支清理；妄想测试失败继续修改。

declaredIntent.summary: Add a fail-closed, dry-run-capable write planner for Bootstrap adoption.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: dry-run and pre-confirmation produce zero writes
out-of-scope path, traversal, symlink, or malformed marker is rejected
dirty or drifted repository is rejected before execution
non-interactive execution without confirmation is rejected
managed Makefile block is idempotent and preserves project content

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized governance code, tests, documentation, structured decision evidence, PR, merge, archive, close, and branch cleanup.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
